coreIssue: This growth conversation is about whether Ahmed is defining Head of Product success in strategic leadership terms or slipping back into tactical execution.
openingQuestion: How do you define strong next-level performance for a Head of Product, and where does your current work most pull you away from that?
listenFor: whether he names one concrete leadership scope he wants to own end-to-end rather than listing broad traits
whether he pivots quickly into sprint, roadmap, or delivery detail when asked about influence, trust, or strategic decisions
if they identify a specific stakeholder or forum where he feels less credible, less visible, or slower to lead
avoid: do not turn this into a critique of recent tactical choices before you hear whether pressure or confidence is driving them
do not leave with a generic development list that lacks one clear leadership-shaped ownership area
goodOutcome: You and Ahmed have agreed one Head of Product-level scope he will own more strategically, plus the stakeholder influence behaviour that will define success in that scope.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where Ahmed defaulted to tactical delivery and one where he operated at strategic Head of Product level so you can contrast the patterns concretely.
